sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content:
Cigarette smoking (Environmental/behavioral): Nearly all cases of SCLC are attributable to cigarette smoking [MS-3]. Patients with SCLC who continue to smoke tobacco during treatment have increased toxicity and shorter survival [MS-3]. Strength: Primary etiologic factor; >95% attributable risk.
Age (Demographic): The incidence of SCLC increases with age with the median age at diagnosis being >70 years [MS-14]. Age <70 years is associated with favorable prognosis in limited-stage SCLC [MS-8]. Strength: Strong prognostic factor.
Poor performance status (Clinical): Poor PS (3–4) is among the most important adverse prognostic factors [MS-8]. Patients with good PS (0–2) are eligible for more aggressive treatment approaches. Strength: Strong prognostic factor.
Extensive-stage disease at diagnosis (Clinical/staging): Approximately 66% of patients present with overt hematogenous metastases at diagnosis, commonly involving contralateral lung, liver, adrenal glands, brain, bones, and/or bone marrow [MS-6]. Strength: Primary determinant of treatment approach and prognosis.
Elevated lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) (Biomarker): Normal LDH is associated with favorable prognosis. Elevated LDH is associated with bulky disease and is an adverse prognostic factor [MS-8]. Strength: Established prognostic factor.

cards.detail: Nearly all cases of SCLC are attributable to cigarette smoking [MS-3]. Patients with SCLC who continue to smoke tobacco during treatment have increased toxicity and shorter survival [MS-3].
